D1.description: retain field as-is; resolve as EXPLICIT_PRODUCT_DEFAULT convention
risks: does not address demonstrated redundancy with array order
does not address demonstrated disagreement risk

D1.description: remove explicit sequenceOrder; array position becomes sole ordering signal; validator rewritten to use array index
advantages: eliminates redundancy and disagreement risk entirely
risks: touches a published immutable artifact type

D1.description: delete field entirely, no derived-order replacement
risks: worse ergonomics for isolated slot reads
D1.backwardCompatibility: requires explicit handling, same as B

D6_D10.description: relax workout-definition.schema.json required array so components is optional; matches fixture (0/23 EASY_STANDARD, 0/10 LONG_RUN_STANDARD occurrences ever have components)
advantages: matches strongest structural evidence (33 combined instances)
removes need to author non-corresponding value
relaxing a requirement is backward-compatible by construction
risks: needs a principled trigger condition (Task 8), not an ad hoc per-workout carve-out
D6_D10.processBImpact: none currently; future contract must handle absent=no-constraint
D6_D10.artifactVersionCascade: none for schema step; v3 of each workout -> INTERMEDIATE_MODIFIER v3 -> combination v5 if content also removed
D6_D10.effectOnProductionBlockers: resolves D6/D10 only once placeholder content is removed; 2/13 decisions, 2/9 artifacts

D6_D10.description: keep components required, formalize a canonical single-entry continuous descriptor
risks: still asserts structure fixture never produces (0/33 match)
existing value already resembles this option -- risk of keeping a field merely because it exists
D6_D10.effectOnProductionBlockers: could resolve as audit-only but weaker evidentiary case than option 1

D6_D10.description: remove components from all four workouts uniformly
D6_D10.rejectionReason: FARTLEK/THRESHOLD_TEMPO have direct unanimous fixture evidence of real structure (2/2 each); removing catalog-wide would discard real structural information to solve a problem specific to the other two keys

D8_D12.description: retain current 3-part shape unchanged; treat remaining question as vocabulary-precision, not shape
advantages: matches strongest fixture evidence for these keys (2/2 each, always present, always 3-part)
cheapest resolution path if research succeeds
risks: generic MAIN_SET label measurably less specific than fixture's FARTLEK_MAIN_SET/TEMPO_MAIN_SET
D8_D12.effectOnProductionBlockers: resolves D8/D12 as audit-only if source confirms current descriptors

D8_D12.description: expand WorkoutComponentType or add optional structural sub-field with workout-specific main-set subtypes, no concrete dosage invented
advantages: closes granularity gap fixture directly evidences
clearer structural signal for future Process B contract
risks: WorkoutComponentType is part of the stable published boundary, higher blast radius
overlaps directly with D3's ownership question, should be researched jointly
D8_D12.backwardCompatibility: additive enum expansion is backward-compatible; content rewrite is separate optional step
D8_D12.artifactVersionCascade: only if content rewritten: v3 workouts -> parent cascade -> combination v5
D8_D12.effectOnProductionBlockers: resolves D8/D12 only once paired with content update

D8_D12.description: replace inline components array with a reference to a reusable structural template/capability model
advantages: eliminates cross-workout duplication of warm-up/cool-down boilerplate
scales better for future quality workouts
risks: substantial schema/model redesign, new artifact type or shared value-object
much larger migration, warrants dedicated architecture review
D8_D12.artifactVersionCascade: eventual, after redesign, well beyond this Wave's scope

recommendations.architecturalRationale: field redundant with already-present array-position ordering signal; only consumer checks a value-set property fully recoverable from that signal
recommendations.semanticRationale: no domain authority determines which ordinal a role holds; brief mandates shape not order
recommendations.evidence: direct: zero consumers beyond a shape-only validator; zero fixture support for a specific ordering; demonstrated disagreement risk
recommendations.risks: schema/model change touches a published artifact type, needs backward-compatible migration design

recommendations.architecturalRationale: schema-required field with zero validator or runtime consumers
recommendations.semanticRationale: Golden Fixture v3 generated model never structures this key with components (0/23)
recommendations.risks: needs a principled trigger condition, not an ad hoc per-workout carve-out
recommendations.migrationImplications: schema-only step low-risk; content removal cascades to combination v5

recommendations.architecturalRationale: current shape matches fixture-evidenced structure directly, no shape defect identified
recommendations.semanticRationale: fixture confirms real warm-up/main-set/cool-down structure for this key in both occurrences
recommendations.evidence: direct, unanimous (2/2), structural only -- no direct evidence for specific generic label sufficiency
recommendations.risks: generic MAIN_SET label less specific than fixture's FARTLEK_MAIN_SET; may need reshape later tied to D3
recommendations.confidence: moderate (high on shape, low on final label precision)

recommendations.semanticRationale: Golden Fixture v3 generated model never structures this key with components (0/10)

recommendations.architecturalRationale: current shape matches fixture-evidenced structure directly, additionally confirmed continuous-only
recommendations.semanticRationale: fixture confirms real continuous-only warm-up/main-set/cool-down structure
recommendations.evidence: direct, unanimous (2/2); additional structural evidence interval format belongs to separate workout key TEN_K_REPETITIONS
recommendations.risks: same granularity risk as D8; future interval variant should likely be a new WorkoutDefinition key, design note not defect

evidenceInsufficiencyStatement: D8/D12's recommendation covers shape only, with high confidence; it does not cover the specific intensityDescriptor values, for which evidence is explicitly insufficient, mirroring D2/D4's documented source-silence pattern.

sharedComponentsModel.rationale: Four in-scope workouts split cleanly and unanimously by Golden Fixture v3's own generated shape: EASY_STANDARD/LONG_RUN_STANDARD (continuous, 0/33 combined occurrences have components) vs FARTLEK/THRESHOLD_TEMPO (composite, 4/4 combined occurrences have components). Independently corroborated by the already-CANONICAL_CONFIRMED AllowedDistanceAccountingModes split (EXACT_SESSION_TOTAL vs ESTIMATED_SESSION_TOTAL).

rejectedOutcomes.reason: forces fabricated structure onto EASY_STANDARD/LONG_RUN_STANDARD that the generated model never produces
would discard real, evidenced structure for FARTLEK/THRESHOLD_TEMPO
legitimate longer-term direction but larger than what current evidence demands; smaller evidence-matched step should come first
no Process B contract exists to generate into; premature given no Process B implementation

sharedComponentsModel.mechanismRecommendation: condition the requirement on AllowedDistanceAccountingModes (or an equivalent explicit structuralFamily-style discriminator), not an unconditional per-workout free choice

migrationForecast.historicalCompatibilityRequirement: RUN_LAYOUT_4D v1 must remain byte-identical and independently verifiable across all 7 existing releases
full -- relaxing a requirement never invalidates existing documents

migrationForecast.combinationNewVersion: v5 (shared with D1 and any other same-wave change)
migrationForecast.historicalCompatibilityRequirement: v1/v2 of both workouts remain untouched and fully readable

changeTypeSeparation.processBContractChange: none identified for any of the five decisions -- no Process B code exists in this repository to change; D3 (out of scope for this Wave) is the one blocker in the full 13-decision set with a genuine Process B contract dimension; D8/D12 Option 2 would touch shared-boundary territory only if pursued later

acceptanceRuleCrossCheck.D1: field-removal prerequisites (no semantic info lost, no consumer depends on it) directly evidenced; historical-schema-readability and bundle-compatibility require explicit migration design, not yet performed
acceptanceRuleCrossCheck.D6_D10: optional-field path well-evidenced; migration impact now documented
acceptanceRuleCrossCheck.D8_D12: no removal/reshape prerequisite claimed satisfied; remain KEEP_AS_IS-track pending ordinary value research

finalStatus: Option analysis, recommendations, shared-model decision, and migration forecast complete for D1/D6/D8/D10/D12. No classification changed; no schema, model, validator, or catalog file modified.
